Identity.Name: StingEESTypes
Identity.Definition: Essential Electrical Services classification per HTM 06-01 / NFPA 99. Defines maximum permitted changeover time from mains-failure to standby-power available. Drives generator + UPS + ATS sizing, EES validator gates, and the EES one-line-diagram drawing-type pack.

Governance.StandardsBasis: HTM 06-01:2017 (Electrical services supply and distribution); NFPA 99:2024 Chapter 6; NFPA 110 (Emergency Standby Power)

Value.Definition: Type A — Life Safety / Immediate. Sub-5-second changeover. Theatre lighting, ventilator life support, IT for clinical monitoring. NFPA 99 Type 1 equivalent.
Type B — Critical. Sub-15-second changeover. ICU, neonatal, blood-bank refrigeration, key clinical equipment.
Type C — Essential. Sub-180-second changeover. General clinical lighting, lifts, comms.
Type D — Optional. Standby restored within minutes via generator only. Non-clinical / amenity power.
Normal — no standby backup; mains-only supply. Office, retail, catering ambient.
UPS-Only — uninterruptible supply without generator; finite battery autonomy (typically 10-30 min). Sensitive IT loads only.
IPS — Isolated Power System per IEC 60364-7-710 / NFPA 99. Specific to operating theatres + critical care for shock + flammability protection.
